alerts.title: U.S.-Iran kinetic escalation continues into second full day; ceasefire fully collapsed
alerts.summary: U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged sustained strikes for second consecutive day (June 10-11), with U.S. launching multiple attacks on Iranian targets while Iran retaliated against Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. Multiple credible sources (NPR, PBS, BBC, AP, WSJ, Reuters) confirm ongoing dual-axis strikes clustered around Persian Gulf and central Iran (31.7-32.2°N, 52.7-53.3°E). Secretary Hegseth signaled intention to continue strikes.
alerts.region: Iran, Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf, Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan

alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment at 60% U-235; breakout window 1-2 weeks amid active conflict
alerts.summary: IAEA confirmed Iran's uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with breakout time narrowing to 1-2 weeks (Reuters, Jerusalem Post, IranWire). IAEA board passed resolution demanding Iran report uranium stockpiles and grant inspector access. Enrichment threshold poses imminent proliferation risk now concurrent with active kinetic escalation and collapsed ceasefire, creating unprecedented dual-track crisis.

alerts.title: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon kill 17 civilians in Tyre, Sidon
alerts.summary: Israeli Defense Forces conducted airstrikes on southern Lebanon killing at least 17 civilians in Tyre and Sidon (June 10-11), with eight killed in single Tyre strike per BBC and Guardian. Strikes targeted populated civilian quarters, prompting evacuation orders for Christian quarter. Escalation broadens beyond Iran-Israel axis to include direct Israel-Hezbollah kinetic phase.

alerts.title: Iran fires barrage of missiles at Israel; first direct attack since April ceasefire
alerts.summary: Iran launched direct ballistic and cruise missile attack on Israel (June 10-11), marking first such bombardment since April ceasefire framework. IRGC claimed strikes targeted Israeli military installations. Israel responded with counter-strikes on Iranian targets. Both sides signaled escalatory intent if provocations continue. Attack represents critical threshold breach in April detente.

alerts.title: U.S. maintains three carrier strike groups in critical zones; threat level rises to 9/10
alerts.summary: Global threat assessment level raised to 9/10 (CRITICAL — IMMINENT ESCALATION) per real-time metrics. USS Dwight D. Eisenhower, USS Abraham Lincoln, and USS Gerald R. Ford remain deployed in Red Sea, Arabian Sea, and Mediterranean respectively. Three destroyer escort vessels (USS Carney, Mason, Laboon) and USS Florida submarine positioned in Red Sea and Persian Gulf. Brent crude spiked to $92.41/bbl amid escalation; Iranian rial free-market rate at 1.375M/USD signaling economic stress.
alerts.region: Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf, Mediterranean

analysis_note: U.S.-Iran direct kinetic exchange now sustained into day two with collapsed ceasefire, concurrent 60% uranium enrichment at 1-2 week breakout threshold, Israeli-Lebanese escalation, and U.S. carrier positioning at maximum readiness create quadruple-layer crisis with imminent proliferation and regional war risks.
